summary: Issue from /orchestrating: the current full explicit testcase suite is not yet guaranteed to pass end-to-end. Intent conclusion: no new SystemArchitecture.json element or relationship is required; existing explicit testcase baselines and implementation contracts already make explicit tests acceptance boundaries. The quality gate is that every repository-declared explicit testcase must pass before delivery is accepted, and failures must be recorded as evidence for Implementation Design or Coding/Repair rather than handled by weakening frozen tests.

explicitTestcases.name: cli-version-surface
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The packaged local operator CLI exposes the current QwenPaw version without crashing or bypassing the CLI entrypoint.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/unit/cli/test_cli_version.py::test_cli_version_option_outputs_current_version must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Treat failure as CLI/runtime implementation drift; do not loosen the expected version surface.

explicitTestcases.name: agents-list-surface
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The agents list command routes through the shared tool helper expected by the CLI contract.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/unit/cli/test_cli_agents.py::test_agents_list_uses_shared_tool_helper must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Treat failure as command/helper wiring drift; do not replace the shared-helper assertion with a weaker smoke check.

explicitTestcases.name: task-help-surface
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The task command remains registered in the qwenpaw CLI surface.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/unit/cli/test_cli_task.py::test_task_command_registered_in_cli must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Treat failure as command registration drift; do not remove this entrypoint from the explicit baseline without upstream architecture redesign.

explicitTestcases.name: settings-language-roundtrip
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: A settings update can be read back through the backend router with the expected roundtrip semantics.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/unit/routers/test_settings.py::test_put_then_get_roundtrip must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Treat failure as backend settings contract drift; do not mask persistence or serialization defects by weakening the roundtrip assertion.

explicitTestcases.name: git-helper-command-runner
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The git helper uses the shared command runner instead of a divergent command execution path.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/unit/routers/test_git.py::test_git_helper_uses_shared_command_runner must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Treat failure as backend helper boundary drift; do not bypass the shared command-runner contract to make the test pass.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-024-end-to-end-non-repudiation-evidence-chain
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Run tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_end_to_end_non_repudiation_evidence_chain against the real app_server fixture: authenticate user A, delegate a high-risk action through agent/plugin/tool seams, and provide required user confirmation.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: Security Center and the audit query surface expose a continuous user -> agent -> plugin -> tool evidence chain with a durable confirmation artifact digest and no anonymous or fallback-system identity link.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_end_to_end_non_repudiation_evidence_chain must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: The test body and protected harness vocabulary are frozen; fix production security/audit behavior beneath the harness.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-025-audit-integrity-self-healing-lockdown
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Run tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_audit_integrity_self_healing_lockdown against the real app_server fixture: execute a sensitive action, tamper with local audit or settings artifacts by operating-system means, restart or resume, then attempt sensitive work again.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The system raises one business-level data-continuity anomaly, enters local lock mode, refuses sensitive tooling, and exposes recovery requirement through the frozen Security Center backend and operator-web observation surfaces.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_audit_integrity_self_healing_lockdown must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Failure should drive implementation of tamper detection, checkpoint handling, lock mode, and Security Center projection; do not treat missing checkpoint artifacts as harmless cache misses.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-027-lease-expiry-active-defense
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Run tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_lease_expiry_blocks_untrusted_rejoin_until_gap_sync against the real app_server fixture: allow a trusted client to miss the lease window, observe denied rejoin before missing-gap verification, then drive the distinct recovery control point and restored-access attempt.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: Security Center marks the same canonical client UNTRUSTED after lease expiry, pre_recovery_console_status shows denied rejoin, post_recovery_console_status shows restored trust only after continuity validation, and model access remains blocked until that validation succeeds.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_lease_expiry_blocks_untrusted_rejoin_until_gap_sync must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Preserve the two control points and two console observation points; do not collapse denied and restored frames into one mutable final status.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-021-prompt-injection-tool-guard-enforced
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Run tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_prompt_injection_cannot_bypass_high_risk_tool_guard against the real app_server fixture: submit nested deceptive instructions claiming test or maintenance mode while targeting a High risk tool without trusted provenance or confirmation.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The high-risk tool remains blocked at the tool-call boundary, rejection is recorded as durable security evidence with trace-bound nonce semantics, and Security Center/operator surfaces expose the rejected attempt rather than silently executing.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_prompt_injection_cannot_bypass_high_risk_tool_guard must pass as part of the full explicit-test gate.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Fix enforcement at the tool invocation boundary; do not rely on prompt intent classification and do not weaken the rejection evidence requirement.

openQuestions.question: Does this issue require changing design/KG/SystemArchitecture.json?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: No immediate graph edit is recommended.
openQuestions.reason: The graph already contains the security acceptance baselines and the root implementation contract already enumerates the full explicit entrypoint set. The issue is that the declared acceptance gate is not yet green, which is an implementation-design or repair gap unless a downstream agent proves an explicit testcase is invalid against intent.

openQuestions.question: What should count as accepted delivery for this issue?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: All nine explicit testcase entrypoints listed in tests/ARCHITECTURE.md and OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md must pass in one evidence run, with the command, exit code, and failure output captured.
openQuestions.reason: Partial explicit-test success would leave an intent acceptance boundary unfulfilled. The issue wording says full explicit testcases cannot pass, so the gate must be suite-wide rather than scenario-by-scenario.

openQuestions.question: Should the next stage first be Implementation Design or Coding/Repair?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: Dispatch Implementation Design first if the full explicit run reveals missing or stale failure records, unclear ownership, or a need to update ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json; dispatch Coding/Repair directly only after a current failure record clearly maps failures to implementation files without changing frozen tests.
openQuestions.reason: Current design/KG/test-failure-records.json is empty, while the issue claims failures exist. That mismatch is evidence collection and handoff hygiene, not yet a concrete code-repair input.

openQuestions.question: May downstream agents edit explicit testcase bodies or protected harness helpers to make the gate pass?
openQuestions.recommendedAnswer: No, not without an upstream architecture redesign explicitly changing the acceptance baseline.
openQuestions.reason: tests/integration/security/ARCHITECTURE.md marks those bodies and harness vocabulary as frozen business contracts. Weakening assertions would hide the quality-gate failure rather than satisfying intent.

notes: Problem statement: the repository-declared full explicit testcase suite is a must-pass acceptance gate. Any failing explicit testcase blocks delivery until either the implementation satisfies the frozen baseline or upstream intent architecture deliberately changes the baseline.

Acceptance criteria: run all explicit pytest entrypoints from tests/ARCHITECTURE.md in one evidence pass; every entrypoint must pass; failures must be recorded with command, exit code, failing node id, concise failure category, and the first actionable traceback or business-readable failure message.

Architecture evidence commands: npm run validate:system-architecture and npm run validate:handoff:intent must stay green for this handoff; npm run test:argo should be run by Implementation Design if it updates contracts or failure records.

Failure log location: write or update design/KG/test-failure-records.json with the current full explicit-test run result. If raw logs are too large, store only stable failure summaries there and keep raw terminal output or CI artifact references outside the intent graph.

Implementation Design boundary: decide whether current failures require updates to OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, local ARCHITECTURE.md contracts, critical non-explicit guardrails, or design/KG/ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json; do not repair product behavior in that stage.

Coding/Repair boundary: once failure records and ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json clearly identify failing explicit node ids and target implementation files, repair production code under the owning stable elements until the full explicit suite passes.

Do not edit frozen explicit testcase bodies, do not weaken assertions, do not convert real app subprocess acceptance paths into mocks, do not remove failing node ids from the explicit suite, and do not fake pass results by marking tests xfail or skip.

Recommended next dispatch: implementation-design with this handoff plus a request to run the full explicit evidence command, populate design/KG/test-failure-records.json if failures reproduce, and produce or refresh design/KG/ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json for coding-and-repairing.
